The following matters were heard: PTR-16-299774 The Fesko Family Legacy Trust, PTR-18-301520 The Helen Fesko Exempt Trust and PTR-18-301521 The Jack Fesko Exempt Trust.

Appearances: Duane Horning appeared by telephone for David Fesko.
Jon Vaught appeared by telephone for John Fesko.

The Court noted that it saw the notice of the Bankruptcy Court and questions whether or not some of these matters could go forward pending the stay. Mr. Horning stated that at least two of the cases (the Helen Trust and the Jack Trust) are stayed because there are claims against David, that all three cases need to be tried together and that it is his understanding that the Bankruptcy Counsel will be filing a motion for relief from the stay within the next few weeks so that these matters could go forward. Mr. Vaught stated that the Bankruptcy Counsel indicated that he shouldn't file anything in these cases without a further order from the Bankruptcy Court regarding the stay. The Second Amended Petition to Determine Trustee of the Trust, etc., Petition of Ethel Merriman for Accounting and Damages for Breach of Trust, Petition to Invalidate Trust, etc., Petitions for An Accounting to Recover Property Wrongfully Taken, etc. (2), and Petitions for Accounting to Redress Breaches of Trust, etc. (2) are continued to August 13, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the Bankruptcy Court hasn't given a total green light, the Parties are to file something as to what this Court is able to do.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Limit Deposition Subpoena For Consume Records
Off Calendar Apr-19-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearance by any attorney or interested party.

The Court adopts it's tenative ruling as follows: Denied. The Court does not consider new issues and arguments raised for the first time on reply. In any event, nothing would be gained by requiring a discovery request, proper when made, to be reformatted in light of Petitioner's subsequent repleading of its claims across different petitions. Even if the Court considered the merits of the reply argument, however, the requested information is sufficiently relevant. To the extent Petitioner has any legitimate privacy interests in the requested information as against Respondent, those interests must give way to Respondent's right to inquire into the financial history and records.

Judge Peter J. Busch , Clerk:N. Wong, Not Reported.

Off Calendar Nov-14-2017 Continued To Feb-27-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jon Vaught for and with John Fesko.
Amanda Ebey for and with Ethel Merriman.
Matthew Matiasevich for and with David Fesko (Settlor).

Mr. Matiasevich stated that the settlement conference in San Diego did result in resolution, that after that there was some discovery discussion, and suggested hearing John's Petition to Determine Trustee of the Trust and after that set a trial setting conference. Mr. Vaught stated that they need to determine validity of the Trust first. Mr. Matiasevich stated that even if the Trust is invalid, the money still needs to be administered. Ms. Ebey stated that her position is that Ms. Merriman is the duly appointed Trustee and that they have not done mediation. Mr. Matiasevich stated that the Court can appoint Ms. Merriman as the interim Trustee sua sponte. The Court stated that it will not do anything sua sponte, and that it will not set the matter for trial as the parties have not even started discovery. The First Amended Petition to Determine Trustee of the Trust, Petition of Ethel Merriman for Accounting and Damages for Breach of Trust, Petition to Invalidate Trust, etc. and Petition for An Accounting, etc. are continued to February 27, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court expects the parties to get discovery done and that they are going to do mediation. The Court also stated that it would like to see a status report by February 20, 2018 as to what the timeline is for discovery and when they will be ready to go to trial.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

The Court disclosed that Briana Mannion, an attorney now working for Andrew Zabronsky's law firm, had previously been a staff attorney for the Probate Department of the San Francisco Superior Court. He stated that such would have no effect on his ability to act impartially in this matter. No counsel raised any objection to the matter proceeding before Judge Busch.

The Court noted the recent filing of a new petition in the matter reflecting a factual dispute among the parties, and inquired whether counsel had met together to discuss the issues, or to consider the possibility of mediation of the dispute. Mr. Zabronsky stated that no such discussion had taken place. Mr. Vaught stated that his client would agree to participate in mediation. Ms. Ebey likewise stated that her client was amenable to mediation. Mr. Zabronsky stated that he did not yet discuss the possibility of mediation with his client, and that he did not have authority to agree to it at this time. The matters were continued to August 15, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Additionally, the Court vacated the July 18, 2017 hearing date on John Fesko's petition for accounting, and continued that matter to August 15, 2018 so the matters could be heard together.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jon Vaught for petitioner John Fesko; Scott McClain for objector Esther Merriman (by CourtCall).

Mr. Vaught contended that the attempt by the trustor to replace petitioner by appointing the objector Ethel Merriman as successor trustee was invalid under the terms of the trust, as Ms. Merriman would be a "related or subordinate party" as that term is used in the trust instrument.
